refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly focus on the latency metric and end-to-end communication path, which is critical for safety and operator effectiveness.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of the teleoperation communication path under agreed network conditions using telemetry data and network monitoring tools.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with bounded latency and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with low processing delay.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to reduce communication hops and processing delays.
DP3: Optimize telemetry and tasking data formats for minimal serialization overhead.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between network link and teleoperation data flow
Dependency on vehicle communication hardware and software
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates latency control to network protocol and prioritization, minimizing cross-module dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of unexpected network congestion impacting latency.
Assumes vehicle and teleoperation modules support required protocols.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding fallback mechanisms or adaptive QoS to handle network variability.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Realization of teleoperation communication protocols, latency management, and data prioritization.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection carrying teleoperation data with QoS controls.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Incoming telemetry data from Vehicle module for operator feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control signals sent to Vehicle module.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of latency control logic and direct interface with network link.
Vehicle participates in communication but latency control logic is less contained.
Safety Services monitors but does not control latency.
Overall system boundary but lacks direct control over latency mechanisms.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control and teleoperation communication logic are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and maximize ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential cross-module latency impacts from Vehicle hardware or network infrastructure.
Risk of interface mismatches causing latency spikes.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Strengthen interface contracts and monitoring between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear latency control mechanisms and measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Some latency factors may diffuse into Vehicle and Network Link hardware layers.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has strong containment of latency logic, minimizing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified end-to-end using telemetry and network monitoring tools at the Teleoperation module boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add fallback or adaptive QoS strategies in DP to handle network variability.
Clarify interface contracts to reduce cross-module latency diffusion.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, risks related to network variability and cross-module latency diffusion require refinement in DP and IM definitions.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with bounded latency and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with low processing delay.

revise.why: Incorporate adaptive QoS and fallback mechanisms to mitigate network variability risks.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Vehicle and Teleoperation modules support required protocols.
open_risks: Cross-module latency diffusion from Vehicle hardware.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under varied network conditions.
Interface performance validation between Teleoperation and Vehicle.
